title: Write integration test for the full watcher flow

contextToReadFirst.reason: This task verifies every criterion is met end-to-end

tests.name: test_fullFlow_detectsNewFileAfterStartup
tests.description: Create temp dir with two existing .m4a files. Start watcher. Add a new .m4a file. Assert only the new file is emitted.

tests.name: test_fullFlow_handlesRapidFiles
tests.description: Start watcher. Add three .m4a files within 1 second. Assert all three are emitted in order.

tests.name: test_fullFlow_ignoresMixedFileTypes
tests.description: Start watcher. Add one .m4a, one .txt, one .wav, one .m4a. Assert exactly two events emitted, both for .m4a files.

tests.name: test_fullFlow_waitsForSyncCompletion
tests.description: Start watcher. Create a .m4a file and append data to it every 0.5 seconds for 3 seconds, then stop. Assert onFileDetected fires only after the file stops growing.

tests.name: test_fullFlow_missingDirectory
tests.description: Init watcher with non-existent path. Assert startMonitoring() throws. Assert the process is still alive and the error message includes the expected path.

tests.name: test_fullFlow_wakeScanCatchesMissedFiles
tests.description: Start watcher. Add a .m4a file directly to the temp directory without going through FSEvents (bypass the stream). Trigger the wake scan method. Assert the file is emitted.

doneWhen: All integration tests pass. Every acceptance criterion from Story 2 has at least one test covering it. No test depends on network, iCloud, or real sleep/wake — all use temp directories and simulated conditions.
doNot: Use real iCloud sync in these tests — they need to run in CI without network access
Skip the sync completion integration test — this is the most important edge case
Add tests for state tracking, transcription, or the orchestrator — those belong to their respective stories
